objective: Audit the user's project against the internship assignment PDF, verify whether the implementation is submission-ready, identify all gaps, errors, risks, and missing deliverables, and produce a precise remediation plan.
primary_goal: Check the project strictly against the PDF requirements and confirm whether it is aligned with the assignment or not.

context.assignment_summary: Build a conversational AI agent for a fictional SaaS product (AutoStream) that can classify intent, answer questions using a local knowledge base via RAG, detect high-intent users, collect lead details, and call a mock lead capture tool only after all required fields are collected.
critical_constraints_from_pdf: Use a local knowledge base for RAG.
Store knowledge base data in a local JSON or Markdown file.
Classify user intent into exactly: casual greeting, product or pricing inquiry, high-intent lead.
When high intent is detected, ask for name, email, and creator platform.
Call the mock lead capture function only after all three values are collected.
Use LangChain / LangGraph preferred or AutoGen.
Use one of the allowed LLMs if LLM is used.
Retain memory across 5 to 6 conversation turns.
Submit GitHub repository, requirements.txt, README.md, and a 2 to 3 minute demo video.
README must explain how to run locally, architecture explanation, and WhatsApp webhook integration using Twilio webhooks.
expected_submission_artifacts: Core code: agent logic, RAG pipeline, intent detection, tool execution.
README.md with run instructions, architecture explanation, and WhatsApp deployment explanation.
Demo video showing pricing Q&A, high-intent detection, lead collection, and mock tool execution.

anti_hallucination_rules: Do not assume missing implementation exists unless it is directly confirmed by files, logs, or code provided.
Do not infer compliance from naming alone; inspect actual logic or recorded runtime behavior.
When unsure, mark the item as unverified instead of guessing.
Never claim the project is compliant unless every mandatory requirement is checked against evidence.
Do not drift into generic advice unrelated to the PDF.
Do not invent file contents, code behavior, or deployment details.
If a requirement is partially implemented, explicitly say what is present and what is missing.
instructions.scope: Only assess the project against the internship assignment PDF and the provided project structure/runtime output.

execution_plan.pass_model: Each pass must independently re-check the project from a different angle before drawing a final conclusion.

passes.name: Requirement Extraction Pass
passes.task: Read the assignment PDF and extract every explicit requirement, deliverable, capability, and constraint into a normalized checklist.
passes.output_needed: A numbered checklist of every requirement, grouped by must-have, deliverables, runtime behavior, and documentation.

passes.name: Repository Structure Pass
passes.task: Inspect the project structure and map each file/folder to a PDF requirement.
passes.output_needed: A mapping table from requirement to implementation file(s), or a note that the requirement is missing/unverified.

passes.name: Runtime Behavior Pass
passes.task: Inspect demo output, logs, and execution traces to verify actual behavior for intent detection, RAG responses, lead capture flow, state persistence, and duplicate-prevention behavior.
passes.output_needed: Observed behavior vs expected behavior, with any mismatch highlighted.

passes.name: Functional Integrity Pass
passes.task: Check the correctness of the core flows: greeting handling, pricing query handling, high-intent detection, slot collection, tool calling gate, memory retention, and response consistency.
passes.output_needed: A bug list containing logic issues, edge-case failures, and any flow that may cause rejection in evaluation.

passes.name: Submission Readiness Pass
passes.task: Verify whether the repository includes all required submission materials: README, requirements.txt, repo structure, demo video readiness, architecture explanation, and WhatsApp webhook explanation.
passes.output_needed: A submission-readiness verdict with exact missing items or improvements needed.

passes.name: Final Audit Pass
passes.task: Re-read all prior findings and perform a final consistency check to ensure there are no contradictions, unsupported assumptions, or overlooked mandatory requirements.
passes.output_needed: A final verdict: submission-ready, partially ready, or not ready, plus the minimum changes needed.

task_breakdown.title: Parse the assignment requirements
steps: Extract all must-have capabilities from the PDF.
Extract all technical requirements.
Extract all deliverables.
Extract all constraints on tools, memory, RAG, and lead capture.
Extract all demo expectations.
Extract all README expectations.
Extract the evaluation criteria.
acceptance_criteria: Every explicit PDF requirement appears in the checklist.
No requirement is merged away or omitted.
The checklist distinguishes between mandatory and optional items.

task_breakdown.title: Map the repository to requirements
steps: List the repository files and folders.
Identify which file implements each major capability.
Mark any requirement that has no clear file-level implementation.
Note any extra files that are useful but not required by the assignment.
acceptance_criteria: Every requirement has a file mapping or is explicitly marked missing/unverified.
Extra files are separated from mandatory deliverables.
The mapping is evidence-based.

task_breakdown.title: Verify intent classification
steps: Confirm the three required intent classes exactly match the assignment.
Check whether greetings, pricing questions, and buying intent are routed correctly.
Check whether intent changes during a lead-capture sequence are handled safely.
Check whether the model or rules overclassify or underclassify user inputs.
acceptance_criteria: Intent labels are correct and consistent.
Intent routing does not break the lead flow.
No missing required label exists.

task_breakdown.title: Verify local RAG knowledge base behavior
steps: Check that the knowledge base is local and not web-dependent.
Check that pricing, plan features, refund policy, and support policy are present.
Check that responses are generated from the local data source.
Check that the agent does not hallucinate unsupported pricing or policy details.
Check that the RAG path is deterministic or correctly retrieved.
acceptance_criteria: All required facts exist in the local KB.
The answer path uses the KB, not model memory.
Unsupported facts are not fabricated.

task_breakdown.title: Verify lead capture flow and tool gating
steps: Check that high-intent users are identified correctly.
Check that the agent asks for name, email, and creator platform in a controlled flow.
Check that the mock_lead_capture function is called only after all three values are available.
Check that the function is not called early, twice, or with incomplete data.
Check that duplicate lead submission is blocked or handled safely.
acceptance_criteria: Tool executes only after all required fields are collected.
No premature or duplicate lead capture occurs.
The lead state is preserved across turns.

task_breakdown.title: Verify memory and multi-turn state
steps: Check that memory persists across at least 5 to 6 turns.
Check that collected slots survive between turns.
Check that turn count, lead status, and conversation history are stored.
Check that a repeated session does not lose state unexpectedly.
acceptance_criteria: State survives across multiple messages.
Conversation continuity works as expected.
Memory behavior matches the assignment expectation.

task_breakdown.title: Verify README completeness
steps: Check whether the README explains how to run locally.
Check whether the README explains the architecture in about 200 words or more if needed.
Check whether the README explains the WhatsApp webhook integration using Twilio.
Check whether the README includes repository setup and demo instructions.
Check whether the README is understandable to a reviewer with no context.
acceptance_criteria: README covers every required section.
README is concise but complete.
README does not omit the webhook explanation.

task_breakdown.title: Verify demo readiness
steps: Check whether the demo sequence shows the required 4 behaviors.
Check whether the demo demonstrates pricing Q&A, high-intent detection, lead collection, and mock capture.
Check whether the demo output is easy to understand in 2 to 3 minutes.
Check whether the demo is repeatable and not dependent on hidden manual steps.
acceptance_criteria: Demo covers all required behaviors.
Demo is not missing a mandatory showcase item.
Demo is submission-friendly.

task_breakdown.title: Verify technical stack compliance
steps: Check Python version compatibility.
Check framework compliance with LangGraph, LangChain, or AutoGen.
Check whether the LLM choice is allowed if used.
Check whether dependencies are listed correctly in requirements.txt.
Check whether the implementation can run locally without hidden assumptions.
acceptance_criteria: Stack aligns with the assignment.
Dependencies are complete.
No forbidden or irrelevant dependency is required for core execution.

task_breakdown.title: Check for errors and submission risks
steps: Identify logic bugs.
Identify missing files.
Identify broken flows.
Identify unclear README sections.
Identify demo risks.
Identify any mismatch between assignment wording and implementation.
acceptance_criteria: Every issue is categorized by severity.
Each issue has a direct fix.
No issue is hidden or vague.

must_check_exactly: Intent labels: casual greeting, product or pricing inquiry, high-intent lead.
RAG must use a local JSON or Markdown knowledge base.
Knowledge base must include Basic Plan, Pro Plan, refund policy, and support policy.
Lead capture fields: name, email, platform.
Tool call only after all fields are collected.
Memory must retain at least 5 to 6 turns.
README must include local run steps, architecture explanation, and WhatsApp webhook integration.
Demo video must show the exact required flow.
Submission must include GitHub repository and requirements.txt.
critical_fail_conditions: Missing tool gating for lead capture.
Missing README sections required by the assignment.
Demo does not show the mandatory flow.

required_sections: 8. Files to keep / files to remove
evidence_policy: Every important claim must cite file evidence, runtime evidence, or both.
If something is inferred, label it as an inference.
If evidence is missing, say unverified.

cleanup_guidance.objective: Remove or ignore unwanted documents and clutter after the audit so only submission-relevant files remain.
criteria_for_unwanted_documents: Temporary or generated demo artifacts that are not needed in the final submission.
Duplicate prompt files that are not used by the repo.
Local runtime state snapshots that should not be committed.
Build artifacts, caches, and nonessential debug outputs.
Any file that adds confusion without helping the reviewer.
do_not_remove: Webhook example if it is part of the intended submission
Tests and docs if they improve submission quality

final_instruction: Run the full audit in 6 passes, report all issues plainly, and conclude whether the project is truly submission-ready according to the PDF. If any requirement fails, list the exact minimum changes needed before submission.
